findings.message: The revised first viewport is photo-led and now clearly feels like a roofing service page while preserving business clarity and CTA.
findings.evidence: After desktop screenshot: approved roofing image spans the full hero surface with headline, CTA, service tags, and proof cells layered into the composition.
findings.suggestedFix: Minor optional improvement: crop mobile slightly lower to show more roof texture earlier, but current version is acceptable.

findings.message: The design still fits the productized-modern family while feeling more like a local roofing company.
findings.evidence: Clean nav, material proof cells, restrained palette, and full-bleed roof image preserve the family direction.
findings.suggestedFix: Keep this direction for the next iteration; avoid adding decorative complexity.

findings.message: The approved roof image is now the primary visual surface and has much stronger first-viewport impact.
findings.evidence: After desktop screenshot: roof image fills the hero; after mobile screenshot: image remains full-viewport background behind H1/CTA.

findings.message: CTA and phone path remain clearly visible above the fold on desktop and mobile.
Mobile is readable, conversion path is visible, and no overlap is visible in the captured first viewport.
findings.evidence: After mobile screenshot: brand, call button, H1, description, and CTAs are visible and readable.
findings.suggestedFix: Optional crop adjustment to reduce empty sky at the top.

topFixes: Optional: tune mobile hero image-position to show slightly more roof texture above the CTA.
Optional: evaluate whether the bottom proof strip should be a little smaller on shorter laptop viewports.
